worktreeClose.title: Session in worktree with changes
worktreeClose.keepHint: Just ends the session. Worktree and changes stay intact — you can resume later.
worktreeClose.commitHint: Runs git add -A && git commit on the worktree, then ends the session.
worktreeClose.mergeHint: Fast-forward merge into the main worktree. The session ends afterwards.
worktreeClose.discardHint: Resets all changes and removes the worktree. Unmerged commits are lost too.

sandbox.applyToNew: Applies to new claude processes — already-open sessions keep the previous state.
sandbox.failClosed: Fail if sandbox runtime is unavailable

sandbox.platformsHelp: Platforms where the sandbox is enabled. Empty = runtime decides.
sandbox.allowOutsideHelp: Listed commands run outside the sandbox (subject to normal permissions).
sandbox.bypassHelp: Commands that should not run via the sandbox (excluded entirely).

sidebarPrefs.title: Sidebar preferences
sidebarPrefs.perSectionHelp: Values read by each sidebar section (grouping and sorting). Editable directly via each section's buttons; here you can only inspect and reset.
sidebarPrefs.expandedGroupsHelp: Projects whose groups stay open/closed across sessions. Each key has the format <section>:<slug>.
sidebarPrefs.customOrderHelp: Order defined by drag-and-drop in the sidebar. With no entries here, order is automatic (by last activity).
sidebarPrefs.projectSortHelp: Overrides set on each project's header. Without an override, the section's default is used.
sidebarPrefs.noProjectSort: None — all projects use the section default.

jsonEditor.emptyHint: Empty = removes the key from settings.local.json.

vscode.openError: Could not open in VS Code: {{message}}

help.title: User guide
help.subtitle: How to navigate and get the most out of Claude Code Studio

overview.title: Overview
overview.body:
Claude Code Studio is a local dashboard (it binds to 127.0.0.1, no auth) that wraps the claude-code CLI. Think of it as mission-control for several Claude sessions running in parallel, with first-class git worktrees and live panels showing what the model is doing.

What you get over the bare CLI:
• Multiple concurrent sessions with state indicators (waiting, active, standby, closed).
• Worktree workflow integrated into the session lifecycle.
• Plan / Tasks / Diff panels next to the terminal — see reasoning and changes without leaving the view.
• Session cost and quota (5h/7d) always visible in the footer.
• In-app editors for CLAUDE.md, agents, skills, and sandbox.

sessions.title: Sessions
sessions.body:
The left sidebar splits sessions into three groups:
• **Open**: running right now.
• **History**: existed before, can be resumed.
• **Archived**: removed from the default history (still on disk).

Each row shows a state icon:
• Pulsing bell: waiting for user action.
• Green circle: model processing.
• Dashed orange circle: standby (open and idle).
• Gray circle: closed.

Clicking a history row runs `claude --resume <id>` in the matching cwd. "New session" opens a modal where you pick model, effort, permission mode, target directory, and whether to isolate in a fresh worktree. Use the search input at the top of the sidebar to filter by session text or part of the ID.

panels.title: Side panels
panels.body:
The panels button in the toolbar opens a menu with 5 panels that appear to the right of the terminal:
• **Diff**: uncommitted changes in the current working tree.
• **Plan**: the plan Claude is building when /plan is active.
• **Tasks**: TODOs Claude is tracking.
• **Terminal**: extra (non-Claude) shell sharing the same cwd, handy for parallel commands.
• **Worktrees**: full management of the project's worktrees.

You can open multiple panels at once. They split into columns with up to two panels per column; drag the dividers to resize. The layout is persisted across sessions.

worktrees.title: Worktrees
worktrees.body:
A git worktree is a separate working tree (another directory) that shares the same .git/. It lets you work on parallel branches without disturbing the main working tree — ideal for running more than one Claude session in the same repo without file conflicts.

Typical flow:
1. In the New session modal, check "Isolate in new worktree". The Studio creates `.claude/worktrees/<name>` from `origin/HEAD` and starts the session there.
2. Work as usual. The Diff panel shows your changes.
3. When done, choose what to do on close: **keep** (worktree stays), **commit & keep** (commits and closes), **merge** (fast-forward into base + close), or **discard** (wipes everything).

The Worktrees panel lists every worktree of the project, with actions to create, open a new session inside, merge, or remove.

memory.title: Memory (CLAUDE.md)
memory.body:
Claude Code loads instructions from `CLAUDE.md` files at three levels:
• **Global** (`~/.claude/CLAUDE.md`): applies to every session of this user.
• **Ancestors**: any CLAUDE.md in parent directories up to $HOME.
• **Project** (cwd): `CLAUDE.md` (committed, team rules) and `CLAUDE.local.md` (gitignored, personal).

The Memory tab in Settings shows exactly which files the CLI loads for the selected cwd and lets you edit each one in the UI. Remember to add `CLAUDE.local.md` to your `.gitignore` when you create the first one.

agentsSkills.title: Agents and Skills
agentsSkills.body:
**Agents** are subagents the main Claude can invoke via the Task tool. Each agent becomes an `.md` file with frontmatter (`name`, `description`, `model`, `tools`) plus a body (system prompt). Use them when a specific task benefits from isolated context or a different model.

**Skills** are instructions loaded into context on demand. Each skill is a folder with `SKILL.md` inside, optionally with supporting files. Unlike CLAUDE.md, they only load when the trigger matches.

Both have User scope (in `~/.claude`) or Project scope (in `.claude/`). Create and edit them in the Agents and Skills tabs of Settings — edits are auto-saved.

sandbox.title: Sandbox
sandbox.body:
The Sandbox tab configures the CLI execution isolation: network rules, filesystem, ripgrep, and seccomp (Linux).

Four scopes, in the order the CLI applies (strongest first): Managed > Local > Project > User. Each scope writes to a different file:
• **User** (`~/.claude/settings.json`)
• **User local** (`~/.claude/.local`) — personal, not in git
• **Project** (`.claude/settings.json`) — committed, team rules
• **Project local** (`.claude/settings.local.json`) — personal, not in git

Changes only apply to new `claude` processes — already-open sessions keep the previous sandbox.

settings.title: Settings and language
settings.body:
Settings are auto-saved: the indicator at the top of the Settings page shows "Saving…" / "Saved at HH:mm" / an error message.

Tabs:
• **General**: interface language (pt-BR, en-US, es-ES). Your choice lives in your user preferences (not localStorage).
• **Sessions**: standby timeout (how long a session can stay idle before being ended).
• **Sandbox**, **Memory**, **Agents**, **Skills**: see the dedicated sections.
• **Sidebar**: inspect and reset stored preferences (project order, grouping, expanded groups).
